name: 2 colors own attraction
description:
Red and White form groups of their color.

The universe stabilize quickly.

We can see some groups at the border moving which is probably the symptom of a wrapping bug

name: 2 colors own repulsion
description:
Red and White repulse their color.

The universe stabilize quickly.

We can see some groups at the border moving which is probably the symptom of a wrapping bug

name: 2 colors chase
description:
White is attracted to red which is repulsed by white.

We start seeing some chaotic motion, swarms of red are pushed by swarms of white.

Since the only attraction force has a corresponding repulsion force the system remains chaotic because we are creating constant energy and no equilibrium.

name: 2 colors chase - White attraction
description:
From the 2 colors chase make white attract white.

We quickly end up in groups of white which coagulated. Around them there is a border empty of cells caused by the repulsion of red.

The simulation takes a long time to stabilize completely because some isolated white cells keep being attracted by all the red cells around them but when they get close of a white group, the empty border because big enough so that the group attraction doesn't reach the isolated cell.

Eventually they still end up merging when the corona of red around a white group spreads a bit and let them get closer to the group. Then the universe becomes stable.

name: 2 Colors - Vessels
description:
From the 2 colors chase add red self-attraction.

Suddenly we start to see some kind off dynamic cohesion. The red cells attract each other forming groups which attract groups of white cells chasing them.

But since there is no strong structures the vessels are fragile and approaching other vessels tends to create chaos which disperse them.

As time goes by vessels become bigger and more stable.

It seems the only thing which keeps them disassembling are the wrapping inaccuracies.

name: 2 Colors - Red self repulsion
description:
From 2 Colors Chase add red self repulsion.

The universe never stabilize. We don't see the waves of white chasing red anymore because red tends to spread more, which in turns also spreads white more.

Note that add white self repulsion of red gives a similar pattern

name: 2 Colors - White self repulsion
description:
See "2 Colors - Red self repulsion"

TODO Document the differences more precisely. It seems to create a slightly more structured universe but I'm not sure why.

name: Cellular strips
description:
I'm not sure which analogy to use here.

Expands quickly into a stable universe with organic shapes of red-blue and white-green separated by empty strips.

name: Cellular strips 2
description: It seems that the additional repulsions create thinner strips.

name: Cellular strips 3
description:
Shifted the matrix of repulsion horizontally.

There is much more spread (Probably due to the lack of internal group cohesion) to the full universe and that makes much more and thinner paths

name: Cellular strips - Inner islands
description:
White is repulsed by other colors and other colors are all repulsed by white.

This create organic patterns of multi colors groups surrounding white islands

name: 4 colors worms
description:
This universe creates moving structures of red, green, blue, white alternating small groups with lots of cells.

This creates strong worms which often merge together but also sometime split with a larger worm and a small one.

Since the groups are strong the strucutre resist well the wall wrapping.

The universe doesn't stabilize but keep an infinitely consistent configurations.

Starting from center helps creating stronger structure earlier on.

Sometime a worm eats its own tail and becomes a spinning structure absorbing other worms when they get too close.

name: 4 Colors - worms - single stage
description: Looks a lot like "4 Colors - worms" but without enough cohesion to form full worms as they slip soon after they create a single stage.

name: 4 Colors - Slowly merging 2-colors blobs
description:
TODO Improve this description.

Blobs of red-blue and blobs of green-white move around and regularly merge creating bigger blobs. Sometimes they stop moving.

I think it should converge to a still state because there doesn't seemm to have splits but if it does it takes a very long time. The simulation might be artificially kept moving by the inaccurate computations of walls wrapping.

name: 4 Colors - spreading pattern
description: TODO Describe this one

name: 4 Colors - Infinite moving low cohesion groups
description:
TODO Improve description

Green-Blue chasing red-white but with lots of low cohesion groups and probably a more complex relationship to describe.

name: 4 colors - Waves
description:
White is attracted to all other colors which all are repulsed by white.

This creates waves of white floats pushing incoherent masses of red, green and blue.

name: 3 Colors - Stable islands
description:
TODO Find a good analogy

The universe converges to many small islands with a green-white core surrounded by red bands.

Sometimes more complex structures emerge but they are rare they often collapse into simpler forms.

name: spatial repartition
description:
All colors repulse themselves but there is a white -> red -> green attraction chain.

This creates compact groups becoming still

name: Islands
description: Red islands caught in white-green swarms

name: Stripe patterns
description: White stripes incrusted of green interlacing with red stripes incrusted of blue

name: Rock - Paper - Scissors
description:
RPS-like rules which creates alternating stripes of white, red, green.

Converge into a pretty stable moving pattern

name: Crystal
description: A universe very slowly converging into a stable mesh structure.

name: Blue islands
description: Blue gets horded into islands which get stable early while chaotic waves of red  white and green never converge

name: Crystal stripes
description: Strong white-green-blue crystal-like filament with rivers of red and white

name: Moving structures
description: Some complex moving structures emerge before stabilizing

name: 4 colors islands
description:
Quickly creates islands with a thin white band, a red-blue blob and a green last band.

The islands tend to merge over time because the attraction table contains mostly attraction forces and few repulsion forces.

name: Comple moving structures
description:
The universe quickly creates complex moving structures with a white-blue center pushing a green blob and surrounded by low density red circle or semi circle.

The movers tend to merge together so the universe ends up less and less dynamical

name: Complex islands
description:
The universe create moving structures with a band of white cells pushing a circle of white cells surrounding a red-blue-green core.

The structures end up merging together.

name: Moving to filaments
description:
Initially create long moving structures with red, green-white-green, red, blue, red stages.

The structures end up merging in filament-like structures.

name: Complex moving structures
description:
The universe creates several types of complexe moving organisms sometimes very circular sometimes longer.

The organisms sometime merge completely but often just exchange some particles when they merge. This change of particles sometimes put the movers to a rest and sometimes creates new movers.

The universe takes a very long time to stabilize as the energy decrese over time.

name: Complex moving structures 2
description: The universe create several types of moving structures which end up merging and stabilizing into complexe structures

name: Weakly bounded moving structures
description:
Creates white pushing blue pushing red moving structures floating in a sea of white and green.

The moving structures easily split and merge when they touch each other so the universe never really stabilize

name: Stable chaos
description: The universe quickly converge into an unorganized chaos where particles stop moving.

name: Slowly merging clusters
description: clusters of blue-green-white moving in a see of red until they have all merged or stoped moving

name: Slowly merging clusters 2
description: Creates clusters of blue and of red-green slowing merging in a sea of white

name: Complex clusters
description: Green clusters merging next to more complex clusters of white or white-red shells encircling blue-red core. The clusters tend to move before merging and becoming still.

name: Dynamic islands
description:
The universe create islands with blue-green core closely surrounded by red bands surrounded bu thin white bands.

The interesting parts is that the white surrounding bands (and sometimes the red inner bands) oscillate around some islands while the core remains mostly stable, just changing shape.

It also creates some rare completely rotating islands.

name: Stable multi stage islands
description: Islands of outer white, then very small blue chunks circling larger red and green. Sometimes the islands get longer a bit like worms

name: Complex islands 2
description: Forms layered islands of various forms which get stable

name: Self-cohesive RPS
description: Rock-Paper-Scissors dynamics (white chases green, green chases red, red chases white) with self-attraction added to each species. The original RPS produces diffuse rotating waves because particles spread freely. Here each species coalesces into compact clusters before the cyclic dynamics take over, expected to produce discrete blobs orbiting each other rather than spreading wave fronts.

name: Competing predators
description: White and red both hunt green. Green flees both. The twist: white and red repel each other, so they compete for hunting territory rather than cooperating. Creates de-facto patrol zones — patches of white chasing green and patches of red chasing green that can't overlap, with green perpetually squeezed between rival predators.

name: Two independent chase pairs
description: Mostly random motions without clear interesting patterns emerging

name: Bounded territories
description: Cyclic repulsion chain (white pushes red, red pushes green, green pushes blue, blue pushes white) combined with self-attraction for all four species. Unlike Cellular strips which produces soft organic regions because non-repelling pairs mix freely, the self-cohesion here prevents mixing: each color actively gathers into its own monochrome clusters, producing sharper and rounder territory boundaries.

name: Bubble exclusion zones
description: Blue self-attracts very strongly and violently repels all other species, which in turn repel blue. White, red and green are mutually attracted cross-species but not to themselves. Blue should form tight self-maintaining balls surrounded by a mixed-color gas that wraps around them but cannot penetrate — creating a cellular foam of blue bubbles enclosed in walls of mixed WRG.

name: Linear predator chain
description: A non-cyclic cascade: white hunts red, red hunts green, green hunts blue. Each prey also flees its direct predator. Unlike Rock-Paper-Scissors there is no return link — blue is never hunted and white is never prey. The expected dynamics: directional pressure cascades down the chain, with blue gradually accumulating at the end while the other species form a traveling pressure wave behind it.

name: Faction civil war
description: Two factions (white+red vs green+blue) that strongly repel each other across faction lines, creating stable territorial boundaries. Within each faction there is a predator-prey chase: white hunts red and green hunts blue. The faction walls act as mirrors — each territory runs its own independent wave dynamics, with the inter-faction boundary preventing the two chases from ever directly interfering.

name: Three-layer onion
description: A concentric shell structure built from a chained attraction cascade. Red self-attracts strongly and ignores everyone — it forms immovable dense cores. White is attracted to red but strongly self-repels, settling into a uniform ring around each core. Green is attracted to white but strongly self-repels, settling into a second outer ring around the white ring. Expected result: repeated three-layer structures of green outer shell, white middle ring, red nucleus.

name: Various moving organisms
description: Several different moving species emerge through time and move for a long time until they are all merged

name: fast movers
description: large simple organisms moving very fast in the universe

name: spinning structures
description:
create islnds which sometimes have a spinning structure of a white core. 3 groups of red spinning around it surrounded by a circle of green.

Blue is not impacting much the other colors

Both low and high friction produce interesting results

name: zoom 4 stages mover
description: Converges into a layered moving structure

name: infinite moving mass
description: A beautiful pattern infinitely moving. Low cohesion but still some distinctive areas

name: Moving structures 2
description: Change the proportion of blue and see how structures become more structured when more blue appears

name: Rotation cells
description:
Cells with a clustered blue-green center surrounded by alternating red-white borders.

The cells move mostly by rotating until they all merge and converge
